business.headline: Rs 10,000 in PPF or SIP? Which builds more wealth in 10 yearsA simple annual investment of Rs 10,000 may not sound like much, but over time it can grow into a meaningful sum. The real question is where to invest it, i.e., the predictable returns of PPF or the market-linked potential of a SIP. So which one comes out ahead after ten years? Let's have a look!

No LPG or LNG shortage, India expands talks with global energy suppliersIndia currently imports about 195 million metric standard cubic metres per day (MMSCMD) of natural gas. Out of this, around 60 MMSCMD comes from Qatar. However, authorities said India is already exploring alternative markets to reduce dependence on any single supplier.

Sensex surges 900 points, Nifty tops 24,750; Reliance jumps 3%The Sensex ended the session 900 points higher, while the Nifty closed above 24,750, as investors stepped in to buy beaten-down stocks following the recent market correction.

Will Iran tensions shake Dubai's booming real estate market?Rising tensions involving Iran and parts of the Gulf are now testing that perception, with reports of attacks reaching areas of the UAE and raising questions about whether the region's instability could shake one of the world's hottest property markets.

Why do only 20% of women reach senior roles in the same company vs 49% men?Many organisations hire women in good numbers, yet far fewer make it to leadership positions within the same company. The gap raises an important question: what happens along the way that slows down women's career progression?

Adani Total Gas hikes industrial gas prices amid Middle East tensionsThe supply challenges come after attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes by Tehran, which have disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The narrow waterway between Iran and Oman is one of the world's most important energy routes.

Still think rural women aren't financially savvy? See how they manage moneyThe common belief is that rural women are not financially aware. Yet daily life in many households tells a different story. Women are budgeting carefully, building small savings and guiding family spending. Could rural women actually be the silent managers of household finances?

Tax deadlines coming up? Know the last dates for revised returns and advance taxTax deadlines tend to creep up quietly, and many taxpayers realise the dates only when time is running out. If you still need to correct your income tax return or plan to pay advance tax, it helps to know the key deadlines and what options are available.

India back on Russia's oil map as Iran war tightens supplyTwo oil tankers that were earlier expected to head towards East Asia have now switched their destination to India. Data from Kpler and Vortexa show the ships are carrying a combined 1.4 million barrels of Urals crude, a Russian oil grade.

Reliance shares gain 3%: Why is RIL rising today amid Iran warBrokerages said disruptions in the Middle East could tighten the supply of refined oil products globally. This could push refining margins higher, which directly benefits companies like Reliance that process crude oil into fuels such as diesel and petrol.

Rupee stabilises after record low, but oil price swings threaten outlookFor currency markets, the rupee's brief recovery may only be an interlude, as traders brace for more turbulence driven by oil prices and the shifting geopolitics of West Asia.

Sensex, Nifty up: Why is stock market rising today despite crude oil concerns?At 9:30 am, the S&P BSE Sensex was trading higher by 245.39 points at 79,361.58. The NSE Nifty50 was also up 85.50 points at 24,566.00.
Sensex, Nifty open in green as markets snap 3-day losing streak; RIL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 245.39 points to 79,361.58, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 85.50 points to 24,566.00 as of 9:30 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market open higher today?At 8:30 am, Gift Nifty futures were trading at 24,677.00. This indicates that the benchmark Nifty 50 may open above Wednesday's closing level of 24,480.5.

How companies can embrace AI without losing controlAlmost every major organisation now uses AI in customer-facing products. But this rapid adoption has exposed an uncomfortable truth. Governance frameworks have not kept pace with the speed of innovation.

Sensex falls 1,110 points, Nifty below 24,500; Tata Steel tumbles 7%The BSE Sensex fell 1,122.66 points, or 1.40%, to close at 79,116.19, while the Nifty 50 declined 385.20 points to settle at 24,480.50.

Tata Steel, L&T, JSW Steel: Why these stocks fell up to 8% todayShares of Tata Steel, Larsen & Toubro and JSW Steel plunged sharply as global tensions and rising crude oil prices rattled markets. Read on to understand what triggered the sell-off today.

Strait of Hormuz shutdown: Is India staring at an energy crisis?Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil route. Read on to understand what it could mean for India's energy supplies and economy.

Rupee slumps to all-time low against US dollar: Why is it falling?The rupee came under pressure as escalating tensions between the US, Israel and Iran triggered global risk aversion, while rising crude oil prices and a stronger dollar weighed on emerging market currencies.

L&T shares tumble over 7%: Why is the stock down today?L&T shares dropped nearly 7% during the session as investors turned cautious amid escalating tensions involving the United States, Israel and Iran.

entertainment.headline: Actor Shiva Rajkumar backs Kantara's Sapthami Gowda, slams inappropriate filming Kicker: After Sapthami Gowda flagged inappropriate filming angles by a few paps at events, veteran Kannada actor Shiva Rajkumar voiced support and urged photographers and media to respect women's dignity.

Actor and TVK chief Vijay attends wedding reception with Trisha. Watch Vijay and Trisha Krishnan attended the wedding reception of Kalpathi Suresh and Meenakshi Suresh's son. This is their first appearance amid the Jana Nayagan actor's divorce case.

Dhurandhar hits 22 million Netflix views; ranks number 1, ahead of sequel's release Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar recorded 22 million views on OTT, becoming the most watched Indian film on the platform. It also set a new benchmark as it became the ninth most watched non-English film worldwide.

Kichcha Sudeep attends cricketer Vyshak Vijaykumar's Bengaluru wedding. See pics Actor Kichcha Sudeep was present at the wedding of former RCB cricketer Vaishak Vijay Kumar in Bengaluru. As he offered congratulations to the couple, the actor attracted attention with his attire.

Thaman joins Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh for background score Thaman will provide the background score for the upcoming film Ustaad Bhagat Singh, following scheduling conflicts with original composer Devi Sri Prasad. This was confirmed by the film's production house.

Anil Kapoor's Subedaar was initially conceived as a theatrical release Anil Kapoor's Subedaar was initially conceived as a theatrical release, but after creative tweaks and structural changes during production, the film had a direct-to-digital premiere.

Hina Khan shares glimpse of her sweet world with Rocky Jaiswal in new post Television personality Hina Khan recently shared a glimpse of her life with husband Rocky Jaiswal on social media. The candid moment has drawn fans into the couple's everyday world.

Young Sherlock review: An interesting detour into the making of detective we knowRelease Date:Thu Mar 05, 2026 What was Sherlock Holmes like before he became the legendary detective the world knows? Young Sherlock imagines the restless, impulsive mind behind the myth, but stretches its mystery longer than it should.

Shah Rukh Khan's King has every name that is big enough, says Saurabh Shukla Saurabh Shukla said that Shah Rukh Khan's King has 70 per cent of Bollywood in its cast. The film, directed by Siddharth Anand, has Suhana Khan, Deepika Padukone, Anil Kapoor, Rani Mukerji, Abhishek Bachchan and Saurabh Shukla in key roles.

Man posing as film producer dupes actor Varsha Usgaonkar, others of Rs 47 lakh Actor Varsha Usgaonkar is among people who have reportedly been duped in a Rs 47 lakh fraud involving a man posing as a film producer. Police have launched an investigation into the case.

Mrithyunjay kept me hooked till the end: Nani's high praise for Sree Vishnu film Actor Nani gave a shout-out to the new thriller, Mrithyunjay, a day ahead of the film's release. The film, starring Sree Vishnu, is slated to arrive on March 6.

Prime Video, ZEE5 this week: Subedaar, Gandhi Talks, Jab Khuli Kitaab and more Prime Video and ZEE5 have rolled out a fresh lineup of titles this week, including Subedaar, Gandhi Talks, Jab Khuli Kitaab and Young Sherlock.

SRK with family, Aamir Khan attend Arjun Tendulkar-Saaniya wedding Sachin Tendulkar's son, Arjun Tendulkar, recently married entrepreneur Saaniya Chandhok in Mumbai. The wedding turned into a star-studded affair, with Bollywood icons like Shah Rukh Khan and Aamir Khan in attendance. Photos and videos from the grand celebration have since surfaced online.

Krishna's Love Mocktail 3 advances release after Yash's Toxic shifts date With Yash's Toxic being postponed to June, Krishna's Love Mocktail 3 is set to release earlier on March 19, taking advantage of the Ugadi and Ramzan holiday weekend.

Naga Chaitanya's Vrushakarma glimpse sets tone for a dark mythical thriller The glimpse video for Vrushakarma starring Naga Chaitanya and Meenakshi Chaudhary showcases horror and suspense, introducing audiences to a mythological thriller by Virupaksha director Karthik Dandu.

Govinda admits he was drawn to doll-like Neelam Kothari, opens up on their love Govinda spoke about his bond with former co-star Neelam Kothari, recalling his admiration for her and the buzz surrounding their chemistry. His latest remarks revisit a memorable era of Bollywood.

Mammootty and Adoor Gopalakrishnan wrap filming on Padayaatra Mammootty's Padayaatra shoot has been wrapped up. Directed by Adoor Gopalakrishnan, the film is expected to hit theatres in the coming months.

Akshay Kumar vs Shikhar Dhawan: Fun cricket match on Bhooth Bangla set. Watch A BTS video of Akshay Kumar and former cricketer Shikhar Dhawan playing cricket during a break on the set of Priyadarshan's upcoming horror-comedy Bhooth Bangla has emerged online.

Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh to release on March 19, fills Toxic's slot Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh will now release on March 19, taking the slot previously held by Yash's Toxic. The shift also sets up a box office clash with Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2.

general news.headline: Iran warship sent distress call, we deployed vessel, aircraft to help: NavyThe Indian Navy said rescue operations were launched following a distress call from Iranian warship IRIS Dena, which was hit by a torpedo by a US submarine, killing most of the sailors on board.

IND vs ENG Live: ENG (38/2 in 4.1 ov), Bumrah removes Brook for 7India (IND) vs England (ENG) Live Score, T20 World Cup semi-final: ENG (38/2 in 4.1 ov). India captain brought Jasprit Bumrah into the attack in the fifth over and the speedster struck on his very first ball, dismissing Harry Brook for 7. Earlier, Hardik Pandya dismissed Philip Salt for 5.

WFH, online classes: Panicked Pak turns to Covid-era measures amid oil shockThe closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which Pakistan receives a significant oil supply,  has forced the Shehbaz Sharif government to turn to austerity tricks to save fuel. A day earlier, the same cash-strapped Pakistan talked tough on Iran and signalled it may have to join the Iran war.

Live: UAE intercepting fresh barrage of missiles, drones from IranUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Further widening the Middle East military conflict, fighting between Israel and the US on one side and Iran on the other intensified over the past several hours. Iran launched multiple waves of missiles toward Israel as the conflict entered its sixth day, while Israel struck Iranian internal security forces, including the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and Basij units. Reports said preparations were underway in Tehran linked to Ali Khamenei's funeral. The US tested a Minuteman III missile from Vandenberg Space Force Base, and the Pentagon named two soldiers killed in Kuwait. A tanker explosion off Kuwait caused an oil spill, while Qatar evacuated residents near the U.S. Embassy. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for live updates on the ongoing Middle East conflict.

Why putting India in dock over US attack on Iran ship is nonsenseHandcrafted ByOur Editors As India celebrated Holi, the US-Israel-Iran conflict took an unholy turn. The war reached India's backyard as a US submarine torpedoed and sank an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean. The opposition has attacked the Modi government and questioned its silence. Is the blame justified?

Arjun Tendulkar marries Saaniya Chandhok in star-studded Mumbai weddingArjun Tendulkar married Saaniya Chandhok in a star-studded ceremony in South Mumbai on March 5. Several prominent personalities from cricket, Bollywood and business attended the celebrations alongside the Tendulkar family.

India condoles Khamenei's death, Foreign Secretary meets Iranian envoyThis is the first time India reacted to the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed in the joint strikes launched by the United States and Israel. The country did not issue a statement immediately after Khamenei was killed.

Rejuvenated Sanju Samson misses 100, but makes Harry Brook pay for his mistakeSanju Samson continued his brilliant form with an 89-run knock in the T20 World Cup semi-final against England. His powerful innings helped India set a strong foundation after Abhishek Sharma against departed cheaply.

How Rashmika-Vijay broke the Bollywood wedding formulaThe last celebrity wedding that genuinely shifted the visual conversation was when Virat Kohli married Anushka Sharma in Tuscany in 2017. In 2026, Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda have had a similar impact.

What is a torpedo? Does India have these killer weapons?The sinking of the IRIS Dena by an American submarine in the Indian Ocean and off the coast of Sri Lanka marks the first time the US sunk a ship with a torpedo after World War Two. What are torpedoes, and why are these centuries-old weapons still relevant in an age dominated by long-range missiles?

Video: US Army veteran dragged out of Senate for protesting war, gets arm brokenThe army veteran held Israel responsible for the war against Iran and said that America does not want to send its sons and daughters to the war for them.

Actor and TVK chief Vijay attends wedding reception with Trisha. WatchVijay and Trisha Krishnan attended the wedding reception of Kalpathi Suresh and Meenakshi Suresh's son. This is their first appearance amid the Jana Nayagan actor's divorce case.

Is Indian Ocean India's ocean? Why IRIS Dena sinking has triggered a fightThe US torpedoed an Iranian warship, IRIS Dena, near Sri Lanka in the Indian Ocean, sparking an intense debate over India's role and responsibility in the strategic waters. Does the Indian Ocean mean India's ocean? What about India's maritime jurisdiction and its role as the guardian of the Indian Ocean?

How Robotic Knee Replacement is Redefining the Journey Back to MobilityEarly consultation with an orthopaedic specialist is essential. Delaying surgery in severe cases can lead to worsening deformity and reduced functional outcomes.

Bengal Governor CV Ananda Bose resigns ahead of Assembly electionsWest Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee expressed her concern over the decision, alleging that it might be a result of the Union Government's pressure.

Meet Rajeev Motwani, the IITian professor who mentored Google's co-foundersRajeev Motwani, an IIT Kanpur graduate and Stanford professor, played a crucial role in mentoring Google founders Sergey Brin and Larry Page. A leading computer scientist, he helped shape early ideas behind Google's search algorithm while building an influential academic career that impacted Silicon Valley's tech ecosystem.

Arjun-Saaniya's red wedding moment curated by Manish Malhotra, Tarun TahilianiArjun Tendulkar and Saaniya Chandhok tied the knot today in a star-studded wedding. The bride stole the spotlight with her elegant ensemble, while the groom made a stylish statement of his own.

US warns of hitting 'deeper' Iran targets, conflict spreads to AzerbaijanThe US and Israel ramped up air strikes on Iran for the sixth day as Tehran retaliated by raining drones and missiles in Qatar, UAE and Azerbaijan. Here's how the conflict unfolded on Thursday.

Claude hit Infosys and TCS stocks, now SaaS firm wants to hire an AI agent directlyAt a time when humans are finding themselves replaced by AI tools, a US-based SaaS company called RevenueCat is switching gears and hiring AI directly. The company has announced that it is looking to hire an AI agent and is willing to pay almost Rs 55 lakh salary to it.

Time, Tide & Tamil Part 30 | 'Maximum' Guarantee Ramachandran: The Matinee MeteorThis is the classic celluloid Cinderella story played out in real time for over five decades, like nowhere else in the world and history: A tale that needs to be told.

SRK, Aamir to Bachchans, Bollywood attends Arjun Tendulkar–Saaniya's starry weddingBollywood celebs turned up for Arjun Tendulkar and Saaniya Chandhok's grand wedding on Thursday. From the Bachchans to the Khans, the starry noon featured the film fraternity's leading names.

Rs 10,000 in PPF or SIP? Which builds more wealth in 10 yearsA simple annual investment of Rs 10,000 may not sound like much, but over time it can grow into a meaningful sum. The real question is where to invest it, i.e., the predictable returns of PPF or the market-linked potential of a SIP. So which one comes out ahead after ten years? Let's have a look!

Maharashtra transport strike could hit Mumbai, Pune most | Explained in 5 pointsDrivers of commercial vehicles like school buses, trucks, cabs, and autorickshaws have announced they would be going on an indefinite strike across Maharashtra from Thursday midnight. This is to protest what they describe as exorbitant e-challan fines. Here is all you need to know about the strike that might hit Mumbai and Pune the most.

Denied sick leave, UP loco pilot undresses before boss to prove piles surgeryA loco pilot in the Lucknow Rail Division undressed before a senior officer after being denied sick leave following surgery.

sports.headline: MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma cheer India from stands in semi-final against England T20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Former captains MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma cheered for the Men in Blue in the semifinal against Harry Brook's England on Thursday, March 5 at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai

Rejuvenated Sanju Samson misses 100, but makes Harry Brook pay for his mistake Sanju Samson continued his brilliant form with an 89-run knock in the T20 World Cup semi-final against England. His powerful innings helped India set a strong foundation after Abhishek Sharma against departed cheaply.

Melbourne mayhem awaits: What to expect from F1's 2026 season opener Formula 1's 2026 season begins amid controversy over new hybrid regulations and geopolitical tensions. Despite criticism from top drivers and war concerns, the Australian GP promises thrilling unpredictability.

Smriti Mandhana joins Team Barbie with special doll before International Women's Day Smriti Mandhana has been honoured by Barbie ahead of International Women's Day, becoming the first cricketer to have a Barbie doll. She joins a global list of trailblazing women, including tennis legend Serena Williams, recognising her exceptional contributions to Indian cricket.

Abhishek Sharma flops in semifinal as poor run of T20 World Cup form continues T20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Abhishek Sharma scored nine runs off seven balls in the semifinal against Harry Brook's England at the Wankhede Stadium before Will Jacks accounted for his wicket.

IND vs ENG: Suryakumar Yadav unfazed by dew factor at Wankhede as England bowl first IND vs ENG, T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal: India captain Suryakumar Yadav remains confident despite losing the toss as England opt to bowl first in the T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium on Thursday.

ICC responds to Sammy's plea, sends private charter for West Indies to return home T20 World Cup 2026: A charter flight has been arranged for the West Indies cricket team after they were stranded in Kolkata for five days due to airspace restrictions following US and Israeli strikes on Iran.

Lakshya Sen enters quarterfinals of All England Championships India's Lakshya Sen overcame the disappointment of squandering four match points in the second game to dominate the decider against Hong Kong's Ng Ka Long Angus and reach the quarterfinals of the All England Open 2026 on Thursday.

Ellyse Perry to return in pink-ball Test against India? Alyssa Healy reveals AUSW vs INDW: Alyssa Healy provided an update on Ellyse Perry's participation in Australia's pink-ball Test against India, which begins on March 6 at the WACA in Perth. Perry missed the ODIs due to a quad injury.

Afghanistan sack Rashid Khan as T20I captain, announce squads for Sri Lanka series AFG vs SL: Afghanistan have removed Rashid Khan as their T20I captain while announcing the squads for their upcoming multi-format series against Sri Lanka. Rashid was dropped following Afghanistan's failure to qualify for the Super 8 stage of the T20 World Cup.

IND vs ENG Live: ENG (25/1 in 3 ov), Buttler, Brook struggle after Salt departs India (IND) vs England (ENG) Live Score, T20 World Cup semi-final: ENG (25/1 in 3 ov). India pacer Hardik Pandya struck with his very first ball to remove England opener Phil Salt for five, handing India an early breakthrough in the daunting 254-run chase. Earlier, Sanju Samson's sensational 89-run knock, combined with impactful cameos from Shivam Dube, Ishan Kishan, Tilak Varma and Hardik Pandya, powered India to a record total of 253 for 7 in the second semi-final against England at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium on Thursday.

India Women eye redemption in pink-ball Test in Alyssa Healy's Australia farewellAUSW vs INDW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India and Alyssa Healy's Australia will lock horns in the pink-ball Women's Test, starting March 6 at the WACA in Perth. The focus will be on Alyssa Healy, who'll play in her last international match for the Aussies.

Sanju Samson's redemption will ease pressure on Abhishek Sharma: Dinesh KarthikT20 World Cup: Dinesh Karthik believes Sanju Samson's form can ease pressure on Abhishek Sharma in the T20 World Cup. The former India wicketkeeper feels Samson's presence at the top could help Abhishek rediscover his rhythm.

Cricket royalty turns up at Arjun Tendulkar-Saaniya Chandhok wedding in MumbaiCricket legends and prominent personalities attended Arjun Tendulkar and Saaniya Chandhok's star-studded wedding ceremony in Mumbai. Guests included Sunil Gavaskar, Rahul Dravid, Anil Kumble, Ravi Shastri, Yuvraj Singh, Virender Sehwag, Zaheer Khan, Harbhajan Singh and Irfan Pathan. Prithvi Shaw and Parthiv Patel were also present, while ICC chairman Jay Shah and chief selector Ajit Agarkar attended with their spouses, making the celebration a gathering of several stalwarts of Indian cricket.

Arjun Tendulkar marries Saaniya Chandhok in star-studded Mumbai weddingArjun Tendulkar married Saaniya Chandhok in a star-studded ceremony in South Mumbai on March 5. Several prominent personalities from cricket, Bollywood and business attended the celebrations alongside the Tendulkar family.

I just want to go home: West Indies coach's plea as team remains stranded in IndiaWest Indies coach Darren Sammy expressed frustration on social media after his team was stranded in India following flight cancellations due to the ongoing West Asia conflict.

MS Dhoni arrives with wife Sakshi at Arjun Tendulkar-Saaniya Chandhok weddingMS Dhoni attended Arjun Tendulkar and Saaniya Chandhok's wedding in Mumbai along with wife Sakshi. Several former cricketers and prominent personalities from cricket, politics and films were also present at the ceremony.

Harry Brook's Fab 4 audition: Why the England captain must deliver at WankhedeIND vs ENG, T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal: Harry Brook's journey to the Wankhede semifinal represents his biggest audition yet. Like Kohli, Smith and Williamson before him, he needs that one defining innings to cement his place among cricket's elite.

Pakistan player fined after alleged misconduct with female hotel staff in Sri LankaT20 World Cup: Pakistan cricket has landed in fresh controversy after a T20 World Cup squad player was reportedly fined for alleged misconduct with a female hotel staff member in Sri Lanka. The player's identity has not been disclosed.

The night India's World Cup dream died at Wankhede and hubris fell silentIn 1987, defending champions India stood on the brink of history, banners aloft and belief unshaken. But as England played spoiler at Wankhede, confidence turned into heartbreak and stunned silence.

Win the toss, win the match? How dew will impact T20 World Cup semifinal in MumbaiAs India face England in the T20 World Cup semi-final at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium, the toss and the evening dew could prove decisive. Should the team winning the toss opt to chase or focus on putting runs on the board in the big semifinal?

Jos Buttler out of form: But should India be wary of England's sleeping giant?T20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Jos Buttler has endured a disastrous run of form in the tournament but will have an opportunity to make amends when England take on India in the high-stakes semi-final on Thursday at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai.

Amir says things about India to stay in the news: Kaif tears into pacerFormer India batter Mohammed Kaif tore into Pakistan pacer Mohammad Amir for making inflammatory comments about the Indian cricket team. Kaif slammed Amir, stating that he said such things to stay relevant in the news.

Suryakumar Yadav vs Adil Rashid: Ashwin picks key battle for IND vs ENGT20 World Cup: R Ashwin believes Suryakumar Yadav's battle against Adil Rashid could shape the India vs England T20 World Cup semi-final. The former spinner feels India hold the edge at Wankhede but must execute well.

India need Kuldeep Yadav vs England: Vaughan suggests big change for semi-finalT20 World Cup: Michael Vaughan has urged India to consider playing Kuldeep Yadav against England in the T20 World Cup semi-final. The former England captain believes Kuldeep's record against England makes him a strong tactical option.

History meets high stakes in Mumbai as India face confident England in semifinalIND vs ENG semifinal preview: India and England will meet in the third successive T20 World Cup semifinals, this time at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai, where history, rivalry and the pressure of a place in the final converge.

Varun Chakravarthy's ultimate test: Is the spin chakravyuh finally cracking?T20 World Cup: For a long time, it seemed like Varun Chakravarthy was India's most lethal weapon in a T20I. However, a poor outing the Super 8 of the T20 World Cup 2026 leaves the spinner searching for confidence and a bit of spark heading into the semi-final.

India series helped New Zealand a lot in T20 World Cup preparations: Finn AllenT20 World Cup, IND vs NZ: Finn Allen credited New Zealand's pre-World Cup T20I series in India for preparing the team for subcontinental conditions after their dominant nine-wicket semi-final win over South Africa in Kolkata.

South Africa coach's hilarious take on 'chokers' tag: You need a sniff to chokeSouth Africa suffered a heavy nine-wicket defeat to New Zealand in the T20 World Cup semi-final at Eden Gardens. The loss ended their unbeaten run and reignited debate over their performance in knockout matches. However, Shukri Conrad had a rather hilarious take on the choker tag.

India are the favourites, but T20 is a fickle game: Harry Brook before semisT20 World Cup 2026, IND vs ENG: Speaking to reporters, England skipper Harry Brook acknowledged that India would start as favourites in the semi-final at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai, especially with home support behind them. However, he also said that T20 cricket is an unpredictable format where momentum can shift rapidly within a matter of overs.

Aiden Markram laments poor batting approach after semi-final exit vs New ZealandSouth Africa captain Aiden Markram blamed his team's batting approach after their semi-final loss to New Zealand. Finn Allen's record century sealed New Zealand's spot in the T20 World Cup final.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
